aiox-master.description: 👑 Orion — Master Orchestrator & Framework Developer. Use for comprehensive cross-domain expertise, framework component creation, workflow orchestration.

aiox-orchestrator.description: 🔄 Workflow Orchestrator & Team Coordinator. Use for multi-agent workflow execution and development cycle orchestration.

aiox-analyst.description: 🔍 Atlas — Business Analyst. Use for market research, competitive analysis, user research, brainstorming, and feasibility studies.

aiox-pm.description: 📋 Morgan — Product Manager. Use for PRD creation, epic management, product strategy, feature prioritization, and roadmap planning.

aiox-architect.description: 🏛️ Aria — System Architect. Use for system architecture, tech stack selection, API design, security architecture, and deployment strategy.

aiox-ux.description: 🎨 Uma — UX/UI Designer & Design System Architect. Use for user research, wireframes, design systems, and token extraction.

aiox-sm.description: 🌊 River — Scrum Master. Use for user story creation, story validation, acceptance criteria, sprint planning, and backlog grooming.

aiox-dev.description: 💻 Dex — Full Stack Developer. Use for code implementation, debugging, refactoring, story development, and autonomous builds.

aiox-qa.description: ✅ Quinn — Test Architect & Quality Advisor. Use for code review, quality gates, test architecture, risk assessment, and quality advisory.

aiox-po.description: 🎯 Pax — Product Owner. Use for backlog management, story refinement, acceptance criteria, sprint planning, and prioritization.

aiox-data-engineer.description: 📊 Dara — Database Architect & Operations Engineer. Use for database design, schema, migrations, query optimization, and data modeling.

aiox-devops.description: ⚡ Gage — DevOps & Git Ops Specialist. Repository ops, CI/CD, quality gates, push. ONLY agent authorized to push to remote.

aiox-help.description: Show AIOX framework help — agent list, commands, and key resources
aiox-help.template: Show the AIOX help overview. Include: 1. Available AIOX agents and their roles (list all 12). 2. How to @-mention an agent: @aiox-dev, @aiox-qa, etc. 3. Available custom commands: /aiox-workflow, /aiox-story, /aiox-init. 4. Where to find tasks: .aiox-core/development/tasks/. 5. Where to find workflows: .aiox-core/development/workflows/. 6. Key convention: all agent commands use * prefix. 7. The core config file: .aiox-core/core-config.yaml. Read .opencode/AGENTS.md for the full reference.

aiox-init.description: Initialize or verify AIOX framework installation
aiox-init.template: Initialize AIOX in the current project: 1. Read .aiox-core/core-config.yaml to check if already initialized. 2. If not initialized, guide the user to run npx aiox-core install. 3. If initialized, show current config: project type, version, user profile, IDE configs. 4. Check .aiox-core/ directory structure is complete. 5. Check .agent/workflows/ has all agent activation files. 6. Run health check: verify Node.js >= 18, npm >= 9. 7. Show summary of findings and recommendations.

aiox-story.description: Create, manage, or validate AIOX user stories
aiox-story.template: Manage AIOX user stories. Usage: /aiox-story create --from <prd|epic> --name "title" — read PRD from docs/prd/ and architecture from docs/architecture/, draft story using .aiox-core/development/tasks/sm-create-next-story.md. /aiox-story validate <file> — run validation checklist. /aiox-story list — scan docs/stories/. Default story location from devStoryLocation in .aiox-core/core-config.yaml.

aiox-workflow.description: Run an AIOX development workflow (development-cycle, qa-loop, spec-pipeline, etc.)
aiox-workflow.template: Execute an AIOX workflow. Usage: /aiox-workflow <name> [--story <path>] [--epic <name>]. Available: development-cycle, qa-loop, spec-pipeline, greenfield-fullstack, greenfield-service, greenfield-ui, brownfield-fullstack, brownfield-discovery, auto-worktree, story-development-cycle. Load YAML from .aiox-core/development/workflows/<name>.yaml, parse phases, execute sequentially with proper agent assignments, handle errors per workflow definition, persist state to .aiox/workflow-state/.

loop-architect.description: Ativa o Loop Engineering — ciclo auto-corretivo com roadmap, testes e lessons learned
loop-architect.template: Load the loop-engineering skill using skill tool. Then: 1. Check for roadmap.md, lessons.md, state.json in project root — create if missing. 2. Read current state and next task. 3. Execute the loop: analyze → code → test → self-correct → update state. 4. Run tests after every change. 5. If 2 consecutive attempts fail on same code, halt and ask user.
